column1: # Golden ordering corpus for the NuGet (NuGetVersion) comparator.
# Format: <a> TAB <b> TAB <sign>, where <sign> is cmp(a, b): -1 (a<b), 0 (a==b), 1 (a>b).
# Lines beginning with '#' and blank lines are ignored.
# Pins the comparator's agreement with NuGet.Versioning's NuGetVersion.CompareTo. The signs
# were produced by the comparator under test (which is differentially validated against the
# real .NET implementation over 20k random pairs) and each row exercises a documented quirk,
# so a regression on any of these fails CI without a .NET toolchain present. Extend the random
# differential with `examples/vercmp.rs` against `NuGet.Versioning.VersionComparer.Default`.
# four-part version: a trailing zero revision is insignificant
# leading zeros in a numeric segment are insignificant
# prerelease labels are case-insensitive
1.0.0-Alpha
# a prerelease sorts below its release
1.0.0-alpha
# dotted numeric prerelease ids compare numerically (2 < 10, not lexically)
# build metadata is ignored in ordering
1.0.0+build1
# a numeric prerelease id sorts below an alphanumeric one (SemVer 2 rule)
# identical prereleases are equal
# core numbers compare numerically
# alphabetic prerelease labels compare lexically
1.0.0-beta
